stories.headline: Iran War: Trump Says End to War in Sight, Spurring Market Rally | The Opening Trade 4/15/2026
stories.summary:
US President Donald Trump indicated he may be preparing to wind down the war with Iran, boosting market optimism and restoring some stability to global energy prices.

Peace talks with the Islamic Republic might restart “over the next two days,” the New York Post cited the president as saying. In a separate interview with ABC News, he said extending a two-week ceasefire that was clinched last week after nearly six weeks of fighting may not be nec

stories.headline: US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade
stories.summary: US Central Command say more than 10,000 armed forces members, as well as warships and planes, are involved in blockade.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says war ‘close to over’ as Hormuz blockade continues
stories.summary: US president says second round of talks could be held in Pakistan as naval blockade of Iranian ports continues.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Italy’s Meloni of not having 'courage' to back Iran war
stories.summary: Donald Trump attacks European ally Meloni for refusing to back the war on Iran and for defending Pope Leo XIV, sparking a rare show of unity among Italian parties.

stories.headline: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.summary: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.headline: Iran faces halt to oil production within weeks if US blockade succeeds
stories.summary: Tehran has a maximum of 16 days of storage before it will need to curtail its output

stories.headline: US military says blockade of Iranian ports is ‘fully implemented’
stories.summary: Six vessels turned around when ordered to do so in the first 24 hours of the blockade, Centcom says

stories.headline: Kenya fuel prices rise sharply despite reduction in tax due to Iran war
stories.summary: Diesel prices rise by a record margin despite a reduction in value added tax.

stories.headline: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
stories.summary: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

stories.headline: North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capability, UN watchdog warns
stories.summary: Pyongyang making ‘very serious’ progress on producing weapons, with rapid rise in activity at main nuclear complexNorth Korea has made “very serious” progress in its ability to produce more nuclear weapons, the head of the UN’s nuclear watchdog has said, in another sign that the regime is seeking to use its nuclear arsenal to ensure its survival.North Korea is thought to have assembled about 50 nuclear warheads, although some experts are sceptica

stories.headline: North Korea boosting ability to manufacture nuclear arms, IAEA chief warns
stories.summary: The nuclear watchdog's chief says there is a rapid increase in operations at North Korea's Yongbyon reactor.

stories.headline: Trump turns on Meloni, saying she lacks ‘courage’ over US-Israel war on Iran
stories.summary: The US president says he is 'shocked at her', delivering a blunt public rebuke to one of his closest European allies.

stories.headline: South Korea jails American YouTuber for public nuisance
stories.summary: Johnny Somali sparked outrage after kissing a statue representing World War Two sex slaves.

stories.headline: Berlin conference seeks urgent aid as Sudan war fuels mass poverty
stories.summary: Germany hosts a Berlin conference on Wednesday to mobilise aid for Sudan’s worsening war-driven crisis, focusing on civilian needs as hunger and poverty soar.

stories.headline: Oil prices fall as renewed hopes for peace talks feed a stock market rally
stories.summary: Shares mostly rose in early Wednesday trading, echoing the rally on Wall Street that came as oil prices eased on hopes that the United States and Iran may resume talks.

stories.headline: Private healthcare in the UK: helping or harming the NHS?
stories.summary: Critics worry the country is heading towards a two-tier system. Dentistry is a cautionary tale

stories.headline: ‘Bifs’ replace ‘Piigs’ as Europe’s bond market whipping boys
stories.summary: Britain, Italy and France have borne the brunt of a sovereign debt sell-off sparked by the Iran war

stories.headline: European airports warn new border system causes up to three-hour delays
stories.summary: Operators in 15 countries report disruption as new EU electronic entry/exit system fully comes into force

stories.headline: Trump’s push to cut US rates akin to ‘banana republic’, says Yellen
stories.summary: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh will lack ‘credibility’ in arguing rates should fall, says former Treasury secretary

stories.headline: Evergrande Liquidators Said to Pick State-Owned Tourism Firm for Exclusive Sale Talks
stories.summary: China Evergrande Group’s liquidators have selected Guangdong Provincial Tourism Holdings Co. to enter exclusive talks to acquire a majority stake in the developer’s property management unit, people familiar with the situation said, as they try to claw back some money from one of the country’s most significant corporate collapses.

stories.headline: Oil Market Taking 'Some Comfort' From Potential Talks, Analyst Says
stories.summary: Emily Ashford, head of energy research at Standard Chartered, discusses the outlook for oil prices as Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed and traders take stock of moves to arrange a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran. Ashford speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: He Has a War Chest and an ‘R’ by His Name. Will Enough Voters Like Him?
stories.summary: Vivek Ramaswamy has all but cleared the field ahead of the May primary for Ohio governor, but whether a finance and pharma billionaire is the man for the moment is another question.

stories.headline: JD Vance Heckled In Antiwar Protest at Turning Point USA Event
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance appeared to express sympathy with critics of the war with Iran: “I recognize that young voters do not love the policy we have in the Middle East, OK. I understand.”

stories.headline: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
stories.summary: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.

stories.headline: Queensland ebike laws could cripple Uber Eats, DoorDash and shared e-vehicle schemes, industry warns
stories.summary: Food delivery services say the proposed laws will affect their workers, while shared e-vehicle schemes claim the laws could make them uninsurableFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastLegislation that would crack down on ebikes and e-scooters in Queensland could curtail food delivery services and potentially end shared e-vehicle schemes, industry figures warn.The proposed l

stories.headline: Japan's Cybozu bets on Malaysia, Thailand for software growth, says CEO
China's more-than-decade-late sanctions ineffective as leverage on Japan
EU cracks down on Chinese goods bypassing tariffs via Belt and Road Initiative
stories.summary: The European Commission has targeted glass fibre produced by Chinese firms using the Belt and Road initiative to bypass EU trade defences. However industry and unions warn the measures fall short and imports may continue to rise.

stories.headline: Pakistan hosts thousands of Sikh pilgrims for 2026 baisakhi rites
stories.summary: Pakistan welcomed around 2,300 Sikh pilgrims from India for Baisakhi celebrations at Gurdwara Panja Sahib in Hasan Abdal, about 45 kilometres from Islamabad.

stories.headline: Peru election dispute deepens amid slow ballot count
stories.summary: Peru saw hundreds of protesters gather outside the Electoral Tribunal in Lima on Tuesday 15 April 2026, demanding a rerun of the presidential election after voting disruptions delayed the process.

stories.headline: Crowds in Bratislava reject Fico plan to scrap overseas voting
stories.summary: Thousands rallied in Bratislava and other cities on Tuesday, 14 April 2026, to oppose Prime Minister Robert Fico’s plan to scrap mail voting for Slovaks abroad, warning it could undermine democratic participation.

stories.headline: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
stories.summary: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

stories.headline: Should Europe welcome Chinese investment?
stories.summary: Brussels wants firms opening factories in the EU to transfer knowledge and employ local staff. Will it work?

stories.headline: The mix of chaotic politics and a resilient economy can’t last
stories.summary: The world’s economy has managed to withstand a great deal of chaos, but new IMF forecasts show that things could get worse

stories.headline: S. Korea’s Naver Prices First Euro Bond Amid AI Push
stories.summary: South Korean platform giant Naver Corp. has sold a combined $1.1 billion of green international bonds, including its inaugural euro-denominated bond, as the company continues its bet on artificial-intelligence investments.

stories.headline: Iranian Oil Arrives in India Just Ahead of US Waiver Expiry
stories.summary: Around 4 million barrels of Iranian oil have arrived in India, marking the first such imports in seven years as the South Asian nation races to secure volumes before a grace period granted by Washington expires at the weekend.

stories.headline: South Korea Secures Extra Oil, Naphtha For Non-Hormuz Delivery
stories.summary: South Korea has secured hundreds of millions of barrels of crude oil plus fresh naphtha supplies from Kazakhstan, Oman, and Saudi Arabia, to be delivered by routes avoiding the blockaded Strait of Hormuz, the president’s chief of staff said.

stories.headline: Greek Government to Reappoint Stournaras as Central Bank Chief
stories.summary: The Greek government proposed the renewal of the term of Yannis Stournaras at the helm of the Bank of Greece, in an appointment that would make him one of the longest-serving governors in the euro area.

stories.headline: Commerzbank CFO Says UniCredit Deal Would Not Create Value
stories.summary: Commerzbank AG Chief Financial Officer Carsten Schmitt said a takeover of the German lender by Italian rival UniCredit SpA could impede its ability to support the economy and wouldn’t create value for investors at this time.

stories.headline: Pakistan Secures $3 Billion in Saudi Loans to Lift Reserves
stories.summary: Pakistan said it secured $3 billion in financial support from Saudi Arabia, providing a key lifeline to shore up its foreign exchange reserves and offset a loan repayment to the United Arab Emirates.

stories.headline: Prosecutors Make Surprise Visit to Fed as Pirro Defends Investigation
stories.summary: The Justice Department’s criminal inquiry into the Federal Reserve threatens to delay the confirmation of the next chair.

stories.headline: Air New Zealand's economy Skynest bunk beds set for launch
stories.summary: Passengers can book a four-hour session in the bunk beds from May for Auckland-New York flights but airline cautions against smuggling in childrenEconomy passengers on Air New Zealand’s ultra-long-haul flight between Auckland and New York can book a spot in the airline’s bunk-bed style sleeping pods from May, which will take to skies in late 2026.In what the airline says is a world first, six full-length, lie-flat sleeping pods, are squeezed into

stories.headline: Candidate calls for new election in Peru, claiming fraud without evidence
stories.summary: Peru’s presidential candidate Rafael Lopez Aliaga is urging officials to declare Sunday’s election 'null and void’.

stories.headline: Tokyo DisneySea celebrates 25 years as foreigners increasingly fuel growth
Japan to ban in-flight use of portable power banks from this month
Invesco to enter Japan ETF market amid deregulation push
Soaring oil prices threaten Japan's municipal bus, sewer services
Magyar vows to shut down Hungarian state TV accusing it of 'North Korean' propaganda
stories.summary: Magyar has threatened to suspend the state broadcaster following a heated on-air clash, accusing it of spreading “North Korean-style” propaganda and false claims about his family during the election campaign.

stories.headline: In Annaba, Pope Leo XIV hails Algeria’s small Catholic flock and Saint Augustine
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV celebrates Mass at Annaba’s Basilica of Saint Augustine, highlighting Algeria’s small Catholic community during his symbolic visit.

stories.headline: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
stories.summary: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon hold rare talks in Washington, DC, amid Iran war
stories.summary: The meeting marks the first direct talks between the two sides, though US officials say more time is needed.

stories.headline: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
stories.summary: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
stories.summary: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

stories.headline: Oil Holds Loss on Prospect of Fresh Peace Talks With Hormuz Shut
stories.summary: Oil held onto a sharp drop from the week’s highs as the US and Iran seek further talks to end a war that has brought the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway to a near-halt.

stories.headline: China Evergrande’s billionaire boss pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Hui Ka Yan expresses remorse in trial proceedings after collapse of world’s most indebted property developerBusiness live – latest updatesA former steelworker who rose to become one of China’s richest people has pleaded guilty to charges including fundraising fraud after the collapse of Evergrande, the world’s most indebted property developer.The property group’s founder, Hui Ka Yan, “pleaded guilty and expressed remorse” in trial proceedings at

stories.headline: Watch: What are Harry and Meghan doing in Australia?
stories.summary: BBC News journalist Simon Atkinson explains how the couple's tour will be different compared to their last visit.

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
stories.summary: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.

stories.headline: Resignations of Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales Set Up Special Election Fights in Texas and California
stories.summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California called a special election to replace Mr. Swalwell, while Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas had yet to announce one for Mr. Gonzales’s successor.

stories.headline: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
stories.summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Accuses Biden DOJ of Unfairly Prosecuting Anti-Abortion Activists
stories.summary: The report is part of the president’s effort to claim anti-conservative and anti-Christian biases in federal law enforcement, even as he pushes to wield the legal system against his political enemies.

stories.headline: DOJ moves to undo Jan. 6 rioters’ convictions for seditious conspiracy
stories.summary: President Donald Trump last year commuted the prison sentences of 12 members of the Proud Boys and Oath Keepers.

stories.headline: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
stories.summary: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: US Stocks Hold Near All-Time High on Peace Hopes: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stocks are holding firm near an all-time high following a rally driven by optimism around a potential peace deal in the Middle East, with traders now eyeing confirmation of a new round of talks.

stories.headline: Telix Pharma Upsizes Convertible Bond Deal to $600 Million
stories.summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals Ltd. raised $600 million from a convertible bond deal that fetched more than expected, in a sign of strong demand for Australia’s largest such offering this year. The company’s shares fell.

stories.headline: America’s corporate boards are under siege
stories.summary: Activist shareholders and regulatory changes are undermining their control

stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.
